game_name: The Legend of the Mystical Ninja

element_introduction_order.element: Short-range melee weapon (grey cats improve range)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Three upgrade levels; early players learn timing and range management in safe town exploration section
Resource management introduced immediately; players learn money conservation
Information gathering, mini-game participation, item purchasing; establishes stage structure rhythm

element_introduction_order.element: Mini-games (Gradius arcade, air hockey, trivia, dice, lottery)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Money farming and optional progression; Gradius reference serves as skill-check mini-game
Optional offensive resource; acquired through money or mini-game rewards

element_introduction_order.element: Level 1 Judo (animal companion mount, ramming attacks)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: First special ability; teaches mobility and collision-based offense before reaching stage 2
Applied skill test; players use acquired weapons and items against minor enemies; leads to first boss
Pattern recognition; Ghost Woman functions as tutorial boss to teach dodge and attack timing

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Available from Level 1 in two-player mode
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Cooperative jumping strategy; simplifies awkward platforming gaps by dividing movement and attack control
Offensive escalation; teaches resource management of special abilities
Mobility escalation; expands vertical level design possibilities
Ultimate ability; high resource cost; teaches endgame boss tactics
Advanced enemy type; introduces mechanistic patterns requiring precision timing

typical_relief_triggers: Completion of town exploration phase (safe gathering period)
Defeating boss and returning to narrative cutscene
typical_tension_triggers: Entering platforming combat section after town exploration
Money depletion threatening throwable weapon availability
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Oscillating structure: each stage alternates safe town-exploration/preparation phase with moderate-to-dangerous platforming-combat phase; progression through Warlock Zones creates crescendo tension that resets with narrative interlude between levels

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Mini-games and shops in town sections are optional but incentivized by money rewards; exploration reveals information necessary for narrative but not always necessary for stage completion

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction through Ghost Woman boss (tutorial-like); Oedo and Shikoku stages teach base mechanics with low penalty for death in exploration phases
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalation via Lantern Man and Otafu Army leaders; introduction of Level 2-3 Judo abilities; amusement park anachronism serves as thematic difficulty spike mixing platform hazards with enemy density
difficulty_curve.late_game: Robotic clockwork ninjas in Iga palace; Sasuke boss fight; Dragon Pond sequence requires mastery of all Judo levels; culmination in princess rescue quest demands integrated skill application
notable_spikes: Otafu Army HQ invasion (thematic jump in enemy complexity and speed)
Iga palace robotic ninjas (mechanical pattern-based enemies)
Final sequence through Dragon Pond and White Mirror challenge

notable_level_patterns.description: Every main stage divides into town exploration (information/resource gathering) followed by platforming-combat finale. Town phase is safe and optional-content rich; combat phase applies learned abilities against bosses. Pattern resets between levels via narrative scene.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Level 1 (Oedo) and Level 2 (Shikoku Island) exemplify this structure most clearly

notable_level_patterns.description: Judo levels introduced sequentially (Level 1: mount, Level 2: screen-clear, Level 3: flight, Level 4: power attack). Each new ability immediately addresses design constraints from previous level, expanding what platforming challenges are possible.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Level 3 (Awaji) introduces vertical challenges that justify Level 3 Judo flight; Level 4+ bosses require Level 4 Judo spam strategy

notable_level_patterns.description: Settings progress from historical/grounded (Oedo temple, Shikoku village) to surreal/anachronistic (Hyotoko dancers, amusement park, clockwork ninjas, Dragon Pond). Tonal shift signals difficulty and narrative escalation simultaneously.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Progression from Oedo → Shikoku → Awaji (amusement park) → Iga (robotic palace) demonstrates deliberate thematic chaos increase

notable_level_patterns.description: In two-player mode, specific platforming sequences designed to require one player to land jumps while other controls attacks. Pattern teaches division-of-labor mechanics and creates puzzle moments distinct from single-player experience.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Referenced generically; applies to 'areas with large or awkward jumps' across stages 1-5
